residual_risk: Persisting stdout/stderr can grow run JSON size or retain sensitive command output. This is non-blocking for this change because the controller already exposes the same payload in immediate responses; output redaction/truncation can be a later hardening task.
stress_test: A failing runner returns only one failed quality gate result; tests confirm the final failed run keeps exactly that partial result and does not create a release pointer or advance branch head. This covers the most important auditability failure mode: a stopped release still has durable step evidence.

P000.description: Release Controller currently returns `PlanExecutionResult` in the immediate trigger/promotion/rollback HTTP response, but the persisted `ReleaseRun` record only stores the command plan and final status/failure. After the request completes, `/v1/runs/{run_id}` and `/v1/status` cannot show per-step exit codes, skipped flags, stdout, or stderr. This weakens CI/CD auditability, especially for quality-gate runs.
success_criteria: `ReleaseRun` persists a serialized `PlanExecutionResult` for completed runs.
Stored run JSON round-trips existing and new run records without breaking old records that lack execution results.
`/v1/runs/{run_id}`, `/v1/runs`, and `/v1/status` include the persisted execution result for new runs.
Failed runs persist the partial execution result up to the failed step.
Dry-run runs persist skipped execution results.
Tests cover serialization, state round-trip, service response, poller persistence, and failure persistence.
The change is deployed to the API-host Release Controller and verified with a dry-run or safe run.

acceptance_criteria: `ReleaseRun.to_mapping()` includes `execution_result` when present.
`ReleaseRun.from_mapping()` accepts old records without `execution_result` and new records with it.
`execute_planned_release()` writes final runs with execution results for success, failure, and dry-run.
API endpoints that return runs expose `execution_result` from persisted state.
Tests cover success, failure, dry-run, and backward-compatible old records.
API-host Release Controller is upgraded and a verification run proves persisted execution results are visible after the immediate response.
assumptions: Persisting the current captured stdout/stderr fields is acceptable for now because the controller already returns them in immediate API responses.
A future hardening task can add output size limits or secret redaction if needed.
T000.classification_reason: This touches persisted schema, execution flow, API/state tests, and remote deployment verification; split keeps code implementation and rollout/audit verification separate.
T000.problem_definition: Release Controller has enough execution data in memory but does not store it in run records. This makes historical run inspection and CI gate auditing weaker than the live response.
T000.proposed_solution: Move or expose `PlanExecutionResult` as a serializable model alongside `CommandResult`, add `execution_result` to `ReleaseRun`, update `execute_planned_release` to store it when finalizing a run, and extend tests for model round-trip, state persistence, service endpoints, poller runs, failed runs, and dry-run results. Then commit/push the change, upgrade the API-host controller image, and verify a dry-run or safe run persists execution results in `/v1/runs/{run_id}`.
risks: Persisted stdout/stderr can make run JSON files larger or expose sensitive output if future commands print secrets.
Moving `PlanExecutionResult` between modules can break imports if not re-exported carefully.
Old run records must remain readable.
T000.title: Add persisted execution results to ReleaseRun
T000.verification_plan: Run release-controller tests, release-controller CI guard tests, root pytest, deploy syntax checks, local model/state/service/poller tests, remote controller upgrade, and remote `/v1/runs/{run_id}` inspection for `execution_result`.

acceptance_criteria: `PlanExecutionResult` has explicit `to_mapping()` and `from_mapping()` behavior using `CommandResult` for nested command records.
Existing imports from `release_controller.runner import PlanExecutionResult` remain valid.
`ReleaseRun` accepts old JSON without `execution_result` and new JSON with it.
`execute_planned_release()` persists the execution result for success, dry-run, and failure.
Run-returning endpoints expose `execution_result` because it is part of persisted `ReleaseRun` mappings.
Tests cover model serialization, state round-trip, service persisted fetch/list, poller persistence, failed partial result persistence, and dry-run skipped result persistence.
assumptions: Persisting the same execution payload already returned by the immediate API is acceptable for this step.
Deployment verification is handled by the separate deployment ticket under the root problem.
T001.classification_reason: Persisting execution_result is a narrow model/state/executor change with clear existing tests and immediate verification.
T001.problem_definition: `PlanExecutionResult` is currently an in-memory runner result and is returned by immediate controller responses, but completed `ReleaseRun` records do not store it. Historical run APIs therefore lose command-level stdout, stderr, exit codes, skipped flags, and partial failure context.
T001.proposed_solution: Promote `PlanExecutionResult` into the serializable model layer beside `CommandResult`, preserving the existing runner import surface. Add optional `execution_result` to `ReleaseRun`, parse it when present, omit it for old records, and write it during finalization in `execute_planned_release`. Extend unit tests so serialization, state persistence, service endpoints, poller-triggered runs, dry-runs, and failed partial execution all prove the persisted field works.
risks: Captured stdout/stderr can grow run JSON size; this ticket only preserves existing controller output behavior and does not add redaction or truncation.
Moving the model can break imports if `runner.py` and `__init__.py` are not kept compatible.
Old records must not fail deserialization when the new field is absent.
T001.title: Implement persisted execution result model
T001.verification_plan: Run the release-controller test suite, targeted model/state/service/poller tests, release-controller CI guard tests, root tests if practical, and syntax checks for touched Python modules.

Remote polling is re-enabled and reports healthy status.
Current prod/staging release pointers are checked before and after deployment.
assumptions: SSH access to the API host and the local registry are available as in prior deployment work.
The existing `deploy release-controller-image <digest>` path is the intended deployment mechanism for controller image updates.
T002.classification_reason: Remote deployment has independent commit, image deployment, and verification gates that should close separately.
T002.problem_definition: The release-controller persistence change is only local until it is committed, pushed, built into an immutable controller image, deployed to the API host, and verified against the remote controller state API.
T002.proposed_solution: Pause remote Release Controller polling, commit and push only the release-controller persistence code, build and push a new `novaic/release-controller` image on the API host from that commit, deploy that image by digest through the existing controller-image deploy path, run a safe remote verification trigger, inspect `/v1/runs/{run_id}` for persisted `execution_result.results`, then restore polling and verify status has `last_error=null` and release pointers remain intended.
risks: If polling is not paused, the old controller may process the new commit before the new image is deployed.
A real poll can update staging to the new commit; this is acceptable only if it is explicit and verified.
Remote config edits must preserve existing branch rules, quality gates, and release pointers.
T002.title: Deploy execution-result persistence to API-host controller
T002.verification_plan: Use local tests/guards from P001 as source confidence, then run remote controller health/status checks, image digest inspection, deployment status checks, and a safe dry-run or poll run whose persisted run record is fetched after completion.

acceptance_criteria: Remote `/v1/status` before the change is saved or summarized with current branch head and current release pointers.
Remote config has polling disabled and `/v1/status` confirms polling is disabled before pushing.
The local commit contains only release-controller persistence source/test files, not unrelated dirty files or problem-ledger artifacts.
The commit is pushed to `origin/main` and the commit hash is recorded.
Remote controller remains healthy and polling disabled after the push.
assumptions: Remote controller config is at `/opt/novaic/release-controller/config.json` and container restart uses the existing Docker deployment on API host.
T003.classification_reason: The task is a bounded publish gate: pause remote polling, commit intended files, push, and verify paused health.
T003.problem_definition: The persistence implementation must reach `origin/main`, but the currently deployed controller still lacks run-record execution persistence. If polling remains enabled, it may process the new branch head before the upgraded controller image is deployed.
T003.proposed_solution: Capture remote controller status and release pointers, disable `polling_enabled` in the remote controller config, restart/verify the controller is healthy with polling disabled, stage only the intended release-controller persistence files, commit them, push to `origin/main`, and re-check remote status remains healthy and paused.
risks: Remote config editing can accidentally drop quality gates or branch rules if rewritten incorrectly.
Existing unrelated dirty files in the local workspace must not be staged.
If polling cannot be paused, pushing must stop until the controller is safe.
T003.title: Pause polling and publish the persistence commit
T003.verification_plan: Use `curl`/SSH to inspect remote status/config, `git status` and `git diff --cached` to confirm the staged set, then `git commit`, `git push origin main`, and remote `/health` plus `/v1/status` checks after push.

acceptance_criteria: Remote worktree is checked out at `745e20cbaefdd12139c344e8838d9e3e0e782ba2` for image build.
A new controller image digest is pushed to `127.0.0.1:5000/novaic/release-controller`.
`deploy release-controller-image <digest>` succeeds and remote `/health` is healthy.
A remote run fetched from `/v1/runs/{run_id}` includes persisted non-empty `execution_result.results`.
Polling is restored with `/v1/status` reporting `last_error=null`.
Prod and staging pointers are recorded after the deployment; any staging movement to the new commit is intentional and visible.
assumptions: Commit `745e20cbaefdd12139c344e8838d9e3e0e782ba2` is available from `origin/main` on the API host.
The existing deploy script supports `release-controller-image` with an immutable digest.
T004.classification_reason: Deployment and remote verification are one bounded controller-image rollout with explicit health and run-record checks.
T004.problem_definition: The persistence commit is pushed and remote polling is paused, but the API-host controller still runs the old image. The host must run a new controller image built from the persistence commit, and remote run inspection must prove persisted execution results are available after the response.
T004.proposed_solution: Build the release-controller Docker image on the API host from commit `745e20cbaefdd12139c344e8838d9e3e0e782ba2`, push it to the local registry, deploy it by digest with `deploy release-controller-image`, then execute a safe remote verification run. Prefer a dry-run trigger first to prove persisted `execution_result.results`; then restore polling and run or observe a controlled poll if needed so branch heads advance under the upgraded controller. Finish by checking health, polling `last_error`, and prod/staging pointers.
risks: Building on the API host can take time and may fail if the worktree or Docker cache is inconsistent.
A real poll will deploy staging to the new commit; if this happens, it must be verified and recorded.
Restoring polling before verification would reintroduce ambiguity about which controller processed the commit.
T004.title: Deploy persisted-result controller image and verify remote record
T004.verification_plan: Run remote git checkout/build/push/image-inspect, local deploy command, remote controller health/status checks, remote dry-run or poll trigger, explicit persisted run fetch, and final status pointer/polling inspection.
